mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically increasing equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 4.5 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, which boosts corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 4.5 percent within a 3-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in industrial production and housing starts signal synchronized real-economy expansion, which validates earnings growth assumptions and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising INDPRO and HOUST fails to predict positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of observed windows over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, rising policy rates increase borrowing costs and compress equity multiples, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of months where FEDFUNDS increases by at least 0.25 percentage points month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating quarterly real GDP growth directly improves corporate profit margins and cash flow projections, which mechanically lifts S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as analysts update earnings estimates.
failure_condition: The rolling correlation between quarter-over-quarter GDPC1 acceleration and 5-day SPX returns remains below 0.10 across all available data periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index exceeds 100, supply-side capacity utilization reaches optimal levels, reducing input cost inflation and supporting equity valuations, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero or negative in more than 50% of trading windows following an INDPRO breakout above 100.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent rises in total nonfarm payrolls and declines in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor demand, which sustains consumer spending and equity earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to outperform a random baseline in predicting 60-day SPX returns across three consecutive rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, rising revolving credit balances indicate pent-up consumer demand being released, which rapidly boosts retail and service sector revenues and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns show no significant positive skew when RSAFS increases by more than 2 percent month-over-month during the specified regime.
